steps.title: Use the parallel lines
steps.content: Because $AE$ and $BD$ are parallel, corresponding angles formed by transversal $CE$ are equal. The angle at $E$ in triangle $ACE$ equals the given $58^\circ$ angle.

steps.title: Use the triangle sum
steps.content: In triangle $ACE$, the angles sum to $180^\circ$. The remaining angle is found by subtracting the two known angles:$$180^\circ-62^\circ-58^\circ=60^\circ$$The answer is B.
